title: strychnos polyantha
intro: Strychnos polyantha, also known as the Strychnine Tree or Poison Tree, is a plant species in the family Loganiaceae. It is a deciduous tree native to tropical Africa, containing the toxic alkaloid strychnine, which has been used in medicine for centuries. The medicinal properties of Strychnos polyantha include its use as a cardiac stimulant, and it has been used to treat conditions such as epilepsy and rheumatism. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to create tonics and elixirs, although caution is advised due to its toxicity, and it is typically used in small, controlled quantities under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
